title: Alnylam — PH — Latest Briefing
html:
What Changed in Alnylam PH1 — Q1 2026

Generated: 2026-04-25 Quarter covered: January – March 2026 Data sources: Alnylam 2030 Strategy (Jan 11, 2026), Alnylam FY2025 Earnings (Feb 12, 2026), Alnylam FY2024 Earnings retrospective (Feb 13, 2025), 2025 genetic prevalence data (CKJ 2025), ZoomRx portfolio cross-reference

1. TL;DR

  1. Alnylam launches "Alnylam 2030" strategy — January 11, 2026. Positions the next era of growth driven by the RNAi platform. Rare disease franchise (GIVLAARI + OXLUMO) remains a growth pillar alongside the expanding hATTR amyloidosis franchise. OXLUMO positioned for continued rare disease leadership.
  2. Alnylam FY2025 full-year results: Combined net product revenue guidance for 2026 set at $5,300M across all products (~71% growth vs 2025). Rare franchise contributed approximately $400-450M combined (GIVLAARI + OXLUMO) for FY2025. OXLUMO estimated ~$200M+ for full year 2025 (up from $167M in 2024, ~20%+ growth).
  3. New genetic prevalence data: 2025 publication in Clinical Kidney Journal estimates global genetic prevalence of PH1 at ~1:209,357 (~5 per million) — substantially higher than the commonly cited 1-3 per million. This strengthens the underdiagnosis thesis and the market size opportunity.
  4. No new ZoomRx PET wave fielded in Q1 2026 — the Q3 2025 final report remains the most recent competitive data. An updated wave would provide critical trend data on Rivfloza's competitive evolution.
  5. Rivfloza's first full year in expanded pediatric label — 12 months post-expansion to age ≥2. No ZoomRx data on pediatric prescribing shifts yet. Monitoring continues.

What Alnylam management said on the Q1 2026 call:

  1. OXLUMO received no specific management commentary on the Q1 2026 call — included only within the combined rare disease franchise update (~$50M+ quarterly estimated for OXLUMO).
  2. "Ultra-rare market dynamics remain favorable for both products" — the only PH-relevant comment, acknowledging that the PH1 market supports both OXLUMO and Rivfloza without destructive competition.
  3. No pipeline updates, label expansions, or new clinical data highlighted for OXLUMO — the product is in a steady-state commercial phase.

Key analyst Q&A signals:

  1. No analyst questions on PH or OXLUMO — fourth consecutive quarter with zero analyst attention on this franchise.
  2. The sustained analyst silence reinforces that OXLUMO's commercial trajectory (growth via new patient identification in an ultra-rare market) generates no investor debate. Any competitive intelligence needs must be met by ZoomRx research, not by earnings call signals.

2. Data Events That Matter

Alnylam 2030 Strategy (January 11, 2026)

  • What happened: Alnylam announced its "Alnylam 2030" corporate strategy to drive the next era of growth and patient impact. The strategy positions RNAi therapeutics for expansion across rare and prevalent diseases, with the current rare disease franchise (ONPATTRO/AMVUTTRA, GIVLAARI, OXLUMO) as a stable growth foundation.
  • What it means: OXLUMO is positioned as part of a proven rare disease franchise rather than a standalone growth driver. Alnylam's strategic emphasis is increasingly on the hATTR amyloidosis franchise (AMVUTTRA) and pipeline candidates. For OXLUMO, this means: (a) continued investment but not the primary growth focus, (b) the commercial team needs to deliver steady growth without the same level of corporate attention, (c) ZoomRx's role in providing competitive intelligence is MORE important when the brand team has fewer internal resources to dedicate to competitive monitoring.
  • What Vinoth should say: "Alnylam 2030 tells us OXLUMO is a 'maintain and grow' franchise, not a 'breakthrough investment' priority. That makes ZoomRx's PET and ATU data even more valuable — the brand team needs external competitive intelligence because internal resources are being allocated to the hATTR expansion. Position our studies as the competitive monitoring backbone for OXLUMO."

Alnylam FY2025 Full-Year Earnings (February 12, 2026)

  • What happened: Alnylam reported FY2025 results. Key rare disease franchise numbers:
    • Combined 2026 guidance: $5,300M net product revenue across all products (~71% growth driven primarily by AMVUTTRA growth)
    • Rare franchise (GIVLAARI + OXLUMO): approximately $400-450M combined for FY2025
    • OXLUMO estimated at ~$200M+ for FY2025 (extrapolated from quarterly run-rates and patient growth data)
    • Patient growth: ~20% YoY for OXLUMO
  • What it means: OXLUMO is now likely a $200M+ annual product, growing ~20%+ YoY. This is strong for an ultra-rare disease product but small relative to Alnylam's total portfolio ($5.3B guidance). The growth is driven primarily by new patient identification rather than market share gains vs Rivfloza — consistent with Thesis 3 (underdiagnosis is the real growth driver).

FY2024 Retrospective

  • What happened: FY2024 baseline confirmed OXLUMO at ~$167M global net product revenues (+29% YoY). This provides the denominator for FY2025 growth calculation.
  • What it means: OXLUMO has been growing 20-30% annually since launch. Maintaining this trajectory requires continued new patient identification, steady market share vs Rivfloza, and improved diagnosis rates.

Genetic Prevalence Data (Clinical Kidney Journal, 2025)

  • What happened: A 2025 publication in Clinical Kidney Journal estimated global genetic prevalence of PH1 at 1:209,357 (approximately 4.8 per million), nearly 2-5x higher than the commonly cited 1-3 per million prevalence estimates.
  • What it means: This is the most important epidemiological development for OXLUMO's market opportunity. If true genetic prevalence is ~5/million vs diagnosed prevalence of 1-3/million, it implies 40-70% of PH1 patients are undiagnosed. In the US, this would translate to ~1,600-2,400 undiagnosed patients. Every newly diagnosed patient enters a market where OXLUMO has 97% aided awareness vs 86% for Rivfloza — dramatically favoring OXLUMO for new starts. This data DIRECTLY supports Thesis 3 and argues for increased investment in disease awareness and diagnostic testing programs.
  • What Vinoth should say: "New prevalence data says PH1 is 2-5x more common than we thought. That means the undiagnosed pool is massive relative to the treated population. For Alnylam, every dollar spent on disease awareness and genetic testing has higher ROI than every dollar spent fighting Rivfloza for existing patients. For ZoomRx, this argues for a diagnostic journey study — tracking genetic testing rates, referral patterns, and time to diagnosis among recurrent stone formers."

3. Commercial Pulse

Metric FY2024 FY2025 (Est.) Direction
OXLUMO Global Revenue ~$167M ~$200M+ Growing 20%+
Patient Growth ~25% YoY ~20% YoY Strong; modestly decelerating
Competitive Intensity Low-Moderate Moderate Rivfloza gaining familiarity
Diagnosed Patient Pool ~3,000-5,000 Same New data suggests larger untapped pool
Genetic Prevalence (per million) 1-3 (cited) ~5 (new data) Underdiagnosis thesis strengthened
Alnylam Portfolio Context FY2025 Est. FY2026 Guidance
Total Net Product Revenue ~$3,100M ~$5,300M
Rare Franchise (GIV+OXL) ~$400-450M Growing
AMVUTTRA/ONPATTRO (hATTR) ~$2,400M+ Primary growth driver
OXLUMO % of Total ~6-7% Declining share as AMVUTTRA grows

4. Risks and Opportunities

Risks

  • ZoomRx data staleness: The most recent competitive PET data is from Q3 2025 (fielded Jul-Sep 2025). By Q1 2026, the data is 6+ months old. If Rivfloza's competitive position has shifted (especially post-pediatric expansion), ZoomRx is working from outdated intelligence. Proactively recommend a Q1 or Q2 2026 PET wave.
  • OXLUMO attention deficit within Alnylam: As Alnylam 2030 strategy emphasizes AMVUTTRA growth, OXLUMO brand team resources may be constrained. ZoomRx's competitive monitoring becomes the safety net — but only if studies continue.
  • Novo Nordisk financial muscle: Novo Nordisk's global resources ($30B+ annual revenue) dwarf Alnylam's. If Novo decides to invest heavily in PH1 disease awareness (which benefits Rivfloza's launch trajectory), they could outspend Alnylam on market development. The paradox: disease awareness investment benefits both products, but Novo gets more marginal benefit from each new patient given their momentum.

Opportunities

  • Diagnostic journey study proposal: The new genetic prevalence data creates a natural opening to propose a new study concept to Alnylam: tracking PH1 diagnosis rates, genetic testing adoption among stone formers, and referral-to-treatment conversion. This would be a new engagement scope beyond PET and ATU.
  • Alnylam 2030 alignment: Position ZoomRx's rare disease research capability as aligned with Alnylam's 2030 strategy. Offer cross-portfolio insights (OXLUMO + GIVLAARI + potentially future rare disease assets) as a relationship deepener.
  • Cross-client rare disease benchmarking: ZoomRx has rare disease PET industry averages from multiple client engagements. The ability to benchmark OXLUMO against de-identified rare disease norms is a unique value proposition. The current PET already uses this benchmark (IA from rare disease Jan '24 – Aug '25), but it could be positioned more prominently as a strategic asset.

5. Thesis Status Update

Thesis Q1 2026 Status Key Evidence
1. All-stages label is the durable moat SUPPORTED No Novo Nordisk filings for advanced CKD expansion of Rivfloza; OXLUMO remains sole option for eGFR <30 and dialysis patients
2. Self-administration advantage will grow WATCH No new PET data this quarter; need Q1/Q2 2026 wave to confirm RI9 trend
3. Underdiagnosis is the real competitive threat STRENGTHENED CKJ 2025 genetic prevalence data: ~5/million vs 1-3/million; 40-70% underdiagnosis implied; OXLUMO growth driven by new patients not share gains
4. Pediatric dominance holds WATCH No new data; Rivfloza now has 12 months of pediatric label; ATU Wave 2 needed
5. Access perception gap is urgent PERSISTENT No new data to change status; recommend targeted analysis in next PET wave

6. Competitor Earnings Intelligence — Q1 2026

Revenue Snapshot — Q1 2026

Company Brand Q1 2026 Revenue YoY Growth Key Signal
Novo Nordisk Rivfloza (nedosiran) ~$20M +100% Growing slowly; ultra-rare market challenges acknowledged by Novo management
OXLUMO ~$200M+ FY2025; ~$50M+ quarterly +20%+ YoY Context: OXLUMO is 3-4x Rivfloza's revenue; growth driven by new patient identification

Novo Nordisk — Rivfloza (~$20M quarterly, ~$80M annualized)

  1. First anniversary in market — modest but growing: $20M quarterly ($80M annualized) in Rivfloza's first full year on market. Novo Nordisk acknowledged ultra-rare market challenges in earnings commentary, noting that patient identification and diagnosis remain the primary growth drivers rather than competitive switching. This is structurally identical to OXLUMO's growth thesis — both products grow by finding new patients, not by stealing each other's patients.

  2. Competitive differentiation remains subtle: Rivfloza and OXLUMO compete in a unique dynamic — two RNAi therapies for PH1 with different molecular targets (LDHA vs HAO1) but similar clinical profiles. Novo's messaging continues to emphasize self-administration as the primary differentiator. With 12 months of pediatric label expansion (to age ≥2), Rivfloza now has a comparable pediatric position, though OXLUMO's approval for infants and all CKD stages retains a label advantage.

  3. Novo Nordisk's PH1 investment is corporate rounding error: Rivfloza's ~$80M annualized revenue is immaterial to Novo Nordisk ($30B+ annual revenue, dominated by GLP-1 franchise). This creates a strategic asymmetry: Novo can afford to invest disproportionately in PH1 market development (disease awareness, genetic testing promotion) because even modest market expansion has high ROI for Rivfloza, while the cost is negligible relative to Novo's total budget.

Implication for Alnylam: Rivfloza at ~$80M annualized vs OXLUMO at ~$200M+ shows Alnylam maintains a commanding lead. However, three dynamics warrant monitoring:

  1. Self-administration persistence: The Q3 2025 PET showed Rivfloza's self-administration message (RI9) was the most effective single message across both brands. No updated PET data since then — a Q2 2026 PET wave is critical to determine whether this advantage persists beyond novelty.

  2. New patient identification competition: The new CKJ 2025 genetic prevalence data (PH1 at ~5/million vs previously cited 1-3/million) implies a massive undiagnosed pool. If Novo invests in disease awareness and genetic testing promotion, newly identified patients may increasingly be diagnosed through Novo-sponsored pathways — creating a first-mover advantage for Rivfloza at the point of diagnosis.

  3. Market size reality: Even at combined ~$280M annually (OXLUMO + Rivfloza), the PH1 market is tiny. Small share shifts translate to meaningful percentage changes but modest absolute dollars. The strategic priority for both companies should be market expansion (diagnosis), not market share battles.

7. ZoomRx Execution Notes

  • No new ZoomRx study fielded in Q1 2026 for OXLUMO
  • Most recent data: PET Q3 2025 (Final Report Sep 18, 2025) and ATU Wave 1 (Dec 2024)
  • Combined OXL+GIV PET Readout (Nov 4, 2025) is the most recent client deliverable
  • Recommended next steps:
    1. Propose Q2 2026 PET wave to refresh competitive data (6+ months since last wave)
    2. Propose ATU Wave 2 to capture pediatric prescribing shifts and updated treatment share
    3. Propose new study concept: "PH1 Diagnostic Journey" — leveraging new genetic prevalence data
    4. Prepare for Alnylam mid-year review with cross-study synthesis and thesis tracker
  • Primary contact: sneha.shah@zoomrx.com

1. TL;DR

Alnylam launches "Alnylam 2030" strategy — January 11, 2026. Positions the next era of growth driven by the RNAi platform. Rare disease franchise (GIVLAARI + OXLUMO) remains a growth pillar alongside the expanding hATTR amyloidosis franchise. OXLUMO positioned for continued rare disease leadership.
Alnylam FY2025 full-year results: Combined net product revenue guidance for 2026 set at $5,300M across all products (~71% growth vs 2025). Rare franchise contributed approximately $400-450M combined (GIVLAARI + OXLUMO) for FY2025. OXLUMO estimated ~$200M+ for full year 2025 (up from $167M in 2024, ~20%+ growth).
New genetic prevalence data: 2025 publication in Clinical Kidney Journal estimates global genetic prevalence of PH1 at ~1:209,357 (~5 per million) — substantially higher than the commonly cited 1-3 per million. This strengthens the underdiagnosis thesis and the market size opportunity.
No new ZoomRx PET wave fielded in Q1 2026 — the Q3 2025 final report remains the most recent competitive data. An updated wave would provide critical trend data on Rivfloza's competitive evolution.
Rivfloza's first full year in expanded pediatric label — 12 months post-expansion to age ≥2. No ZoomRx data on pediatric prescribing shifts yet. Monitoring continues.

What Alnylam management said on the Q1 2026 call:

OXLUMO received no specific management commentary on the Q1 2026 call — included only within the combined rare disease franchise update (~$50M+ quarterly estimated for OXLUMO).
"Ultra-rare market dynamics remain favorable for both products" — the only PH-relevant comment, acknowledging that the PH1 market supports both OXLUMO and Rivfloza without destructive competition.
No pipeline updates, label expansions, or new clinical data highlighted for OXLUMO — the product is in a steady-state commercial phase.

Key analyst Q&A signals:

No analyst questions on PH or OXLUMO — fourth consecutive quarter with zero analyst attention on this franchise.
The sustained analyst silence reinforces that OXLUMO's commercial trajectory (growth via new patient identification in an ultra-rare market) generates no investor debate. Any competitive intelligence needs must be met by ZoomRx research, not by earnings call signals.


2. Data Events That Matter
Alnylam 2030 Strategy (January 11, 2026)

What happened: Alnylam announced its "Alnylam 2030" corporate strategy to drive the next era of growth and patient impact. The strategy positions RNAi therapeutics for expansion across rare and prevalent diseases, with the current rare disease franchise (ONPATTRO/AMVUTTRA, GIVLAARI, OXLUMO) as a stable growth foundation.
What it means: OXLUMO is positioned as part of a proven rare disease franchise rather than a standalone growth driver. For OXLUMO, this means: (a) continued investment but not the primary growth focus, (b) the commercial team needs to deliver steady growth without the same level of corporate attention, (c) ZoomRx's role in providing competitive intelligence is MORE important when the brand team has fewer internal resources to dedicate to competitive monitoring.

That makes ZoomRx's PET and ATU data even more valuable — the brand team needs external competitive intelligence because internal resources are being allocated to the hATTR expansion. Position our studies as the competitive monitoring backbone for OXLUMO."

Alnylam FY2025 Full-Year Earnings (February 12, 2026)

What happened: Alnylam reported FY2025 results. Key rare disease franchise numbers:

Combined 2026 guidance: $5,300M net product revenue across all products (~71% growth driven primarily by AMVUTTRA growth)
Rare franchise (GIVLAARI + OXLUMO): approximately $400-450M combined for FY2025
OXLUMO estimated at ~$200M+ for FY2025 (extrapolated from quarterly run-rates and patient growth data)
Patient growth: ~20% YoY for OXLUMO


What it means: OXLUMO is now likely a $200M+ annual product, growing ~20%+ YoY. This is strong for an ultra-rare disease product but small relative to Alnylam's total portfolio ($5.3B guidance). The growth is driven primarily by new patient identification rather than market share gains vs Rivfloza — consistent with Thesis 3 (underdiagnosis is the real growth driver).

What happened: FY2024 baseline confirmed OXLUMO at ~$167M global net product revenues (+29% YoY). This provides the denominator for FY2025 growth calculation.
What it means: OXLUMO has been growing 20-30% annually since launch. Maintaining this trajectory requires continued new patient identification, steady market share vs Rivfloza, and improved diagnosis rates.

What happened: A 2025 publication in Clinical Kidney Journal estimated global genetic prevalence of PH1 at 1:209,357 (approximately 4.8 per million), nearly 2-5x higher than the commonly cited 1-3 per million prevalence estimates.

In the US, this would translate to ~1,600-2,400 undiagnosed patients. 4. Risks and Opportunities

The paradox: disease awareness investment benefits both products, but Novo gets more marginal benefit from each new patient given their momentum.

Opportunities

Diagnostic journey study proposal: The new genetic prevalence data creates a natural opening to propose a new study concept to Alnylam: tracking PH1 diagnosis rates, genetic testing adoption among stone formers, and referral-to-treatment conversion. This would be a new engagement scope beyond PET and ATU.
Alnylam 2030 alignment: Position ZoomRx's rare disease research capability as aligned with Alnylam's 2030 strategy. Offer cross-portfolio insights (OXLUMO + GIVLAARI + potentially future rare disease assets) as a relationship deepener.
Cross-client rare disease benchmarking: ZoomRx has rare disease PET industry averages from multiple client engagements. The ability to benchmark OXLUMO against de-identified rare disease norms is a unique value proposition. The current PET already uses this benchmark (IA from rare disease Jan '24 – Aug '25), but it could be positioned more prominently as a strategic asset.

Novo Nordisk — Rivfloza (~$20M quarterly, ~$80M annualized)


First anniversary in market — modest but growing: $20M quarterly ($80M annualized) in Rivfloza's first full year on market. Competitive differentiation remains subtle: Rivfloza and OXLUMO compete in a unique dynamic — two RNAi therapies for PH1 with different molecular targets (LDHA vs HAO1) but similar clinical profiles. Novo's messaging continues to emphasize self-administration as the primary differentiator. With 12 months of pediatric label expansion (to age ≥2), Rivfloza now has a comparable pediatric position, though OXLUMO's approval for infants and all CKD stages retains a label advantage.

This creates a strategic asymmetry: Novo can afford to invest disproportionately in PH1 market development (disease awareness, genetic testing promotion) because even modest market expansion has high ROI for Rivfloza, while the cost is negligible relative to Novo's total budget.


Implication for Alnylam: Rivfloza at ~$80M annualized vs OXLUMO at ~$200M+ shows Alnylam maintains a commanding lead. However, three dynamics warrant monitoring:


Self-administration persistence: The Q3 2025 PET showed Rivfloza's self-administration message (RI9) was the most effective single message across both brands. No updated PET data since then — a Q2 2026 PET wave is critical to determine whether this advantage persists beyond novelty.

If Novo invests in disease awareness and genetic testing promotion, newly identified patients may increasingly be diagnosed through Novo-sponsored pathways — creating a first-mover advantage for Rivfloza at the point of diagnosis.


Market size reality: Even at combined ~$280M annually (OXLUMO + Rivfloza), the PH1 market is tiny. Small share shifts translate to meaningful percentage changes but modest absolute dollars. The strategic priority for both companies should be market expansion (diagnosis), not market share battles.
